meta.name: Envision Color Tokens
meta.description: Source of truth for Envision Inc. brand color values. Sourced directly from the Envision Brand Book (2026 Edition). Do not invent new colors, round hex values, or approximate PMS/CMYK conversions.
meta.tagline: Bold, vibrant, and full of life — just like the people we serve.

blue.description: Primary brand anchor. The default lead color for Envision across corporate, commercial, B2B, and federal work.

green.description: Primary brand accent. High-energy accent for CTAs, highlights, and controlled emphasis. Do not use as a full field color.

navy.description: Deep navy for layered backgrounds, footers, high-contrast overlays, and formal/federal work.

bright-blue.description: Bright blue for accents, data-viz, supporting surfaces, and approachable B2C contexts.

forest-green.description: Forest green for rich, grounded surfaces. Useful for mission-forward contexts and dense data backgrounds.

goldenrod.description: Warm accent for highlights, awards, recognition, and human-story callouts.

terracotta.description: Warm accent for energy and approachability. Pair sparingly with primary blue.

violet.description: Violet accent for distinction, specialty programs, and differentiated campaigns.

charcoal.description: Charcoal for dense body copy, dark UI surfaces, and professional contexts where pure black feels harsh.

gray.description: Soft neutral gray for backgrounds, dividers, and tonal separation.

offwhite.description: Subtle off-white for elevated surfaces that need separation from pure white.

black.description: Reserved for print black and logo knockouts only. Do not use for body copy — use charcoal instead.

accessibility.description: WCAG 2.1 AA-approved color combinations sourced directly from the Envision Brand Book. These meet or exceed 4.5:1 contrast ratio. Only use approved combinations when layering text or icons on color backgrounds.

allowed: Lead with primary blue (#003087) on nearly every Envision-produced asset.
Use primary green (#78BE21) as a controlled accent — CTAs, highlights, single-emphasis moments.
Use the extended palette to add warmth, differentiation, and energy to the system — especially in event, program, and human-story contexts.
Use navy (#002855) for formal, federal, or layered contexts where primary blue feels too bright.
Only layer text on color backgrounds using combinations from the accessibility.approved-combinations list.
All text must meet WCAG AA contrast on its background — verify before shipping.
forbidden: Do not substitute primary blue for a navy shade without reason. Primary blue is #003087.
Do not use primary green as a large field or background color. It is an accent.
Do not invent new brand colors. Extend by combining existing values, or escalate.
Do not round or approximate hex values (no #003088, no #78BE20).
Do not convert to different PMS/CMYK equivalents without print-team approval.
Do not use pure black (#000000) for body copy — use charcoal (#53565A).
Do not layer text on background combinations outside the approved accessible list.
